title: May CPI lands this morning with inflation expected to climb to 4.2% as the Fed heads toward a June hold
summary: The May Consumer Price Index releases at 8:30am ET today, with headline inflation forecast to rise to 4.2% year-over-year on energy costs and core at 2.9%. Markets price a near-certain Fed hold next week after May payrolls beat at +172k; Charlotte logs more corporate HQ wins, and fintech M&A heats up.

title: Claude Fable 5 ships to general availability in Claude Code 2.1.170
summary: A one-release window: version 2.1.170 makes Fable 5 — a Mythos-class model Anthropic calls its most capable ever released for general use — selectable in Claude Code, alongside a fix for VS Code terminal sessions silently dropping their transcripts.

title: Anthropic ships Fable 5, its most powerful public model; Google starts an AI price war at $4.99
summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5 — a locked-down version of its frontier Mythos 5 — as the most capable model it has ever made generally available, free in Pro/Max through June 22. Google cut AI Plus to $4.99 to open a consumer price war, Gemini 3.5 Flash hit GA, and a $965B Anthropic anchors a heavy funding week.

title: A security-patch sprint to sell now, a Fable 5 upgrade window, and a retainer the price war makes easier to pitch
summary: This week's shifts hand TSD a rare urgency hook: live Next.js advisories, a record Patch Tuesday, and an actively-exploited WordPress bug make a paid site-security sprint an easy yes. Meanwhile Fable 5 is free in Pro/Max through June 22, and Google's $4.99 price war is the perfect reason to reframe the Managed AI retainer around integration.

title: Record Patch Tuesday lands a wormable Windows kernel RCE; patch your Next.js apps and rotate Supabase keys
summary: Microsoft's June Patch Tuesday fixed a record ~198 CVEs, headlined by a wormable critical Windows kernel RCE (CVE-2026-45657, CVSS 9.8). For TSD's stack: the May Next.js coordinated security release still needs patching where it hasn't been, Supabase legacy API keys are being deprecated, and Nvidia is entering the PC chip market.

title: A hot May jobs report pushes Fed-cut bets back and lifts yields; two big employers pick North Carolina; a strong solar storm reaches Earth
summary: May hiring nearly doubled expectations at 172,000 with unemployment at 4.3%, sending the 10-year yield to 4.54% and cooling rate-cut hopes; SMBC and an Amazon–Corning deal add thousands of jobs in NC; and a G3 geomagnetic storm reached Earth as NASA preps an Artemis 3 crew reveal.

title: 2.1.169 lands: /cd, safe-mode, and a switch to mute bundled skills
summary: A big release after a two-day quiet patch run: mid-session directory switching that keeps the prompt cache, a safe-mode boot for bisecting broken customizations, a toggle to hide bundled skills from the model, plus macOS speed fixes and background-agent env corrections.

title: Apple rebuilds Siri on Google's Gemini and lets iPhones set Claude as the assistant; Anthropic files to go public
summary: At WWDC Apple put a Gemini-powered Siri on every iPhone and opened iOS 27 to third-party assistants like Claude; Anthropic filed a confidential S-1; the Pentagon is testing rivals to replace Claude; and Claude Code stays the most-loved coding agent as the tools converge.

title: A Next.js security release is a paid sprint waiting to happen; the NC AI buildout is your warmest outbound list
summary: This week's shifts hand TSD a fast-cash security patch wedge, a hot North Carolina employer boom to point Grant and Bishop at, and a model-agnostic AI angle straight out of Apple putting Claude on the iPhone.

title: Patch Next.js to 16.2.6 now; Apple ships iOS 27 dev betas and tunable Liquid Glass; Amazon and Corning add 1,000 NC fiber-optics jobs
summary: The Next.js/React security release means Turbopack apps must be on 16.2.6 or 15.5.18; Apple shipped six OS dev betas with refined, user-tunable Liquid Glass; and an Amazon–Corning deal puts 1,000 advanced-manufacturing jobs in North Carolina to feed AI data centers.

title: ISS crew briefly shelters in Dragon over a worsening air leak; two title series swing as Berkshire bets $6.8B on housing
summary: A rare ISS safe-haven order, a record hat trick in the Stanley Cup Final, the Knicks up 2-0, a Sunday severe-weather threat in the Plains, a $52.7M Scary Movie opening, and a Berkshire homebuilder deal.

title: Quiet window: only a bug-fix release since yesterday
summary: 2.1.168 shipped overnight with bug fixes and reliability improvements only; nothing new to act on since yesterday's fallback-models release.

title: Microsoft ships seven in-house models and a 5B coder inside Copilot; DeepSeek takes its first outside money at ~$59B
summary: Microsoft decouples from OpenAI with seven MAI models, DeepSeek raises ~$7.4B, OpenAI rebuilds ChatGPT memory, and a bipartisan bill would freeze state AI laws for three years.

title: A breach week and a real Next.js CVE hand TSD a recurring Site Care retainer; Vercel's Shopify path is a new build-and-own offer
summary: The 2026 breach pattern plus an unpatched Next.js advisory make a managed patch-and-security retainer the highest-fit move; Vercel's Shopify builder and self-revising AI memory open two more sellable offers.

title: Vercel ships a Shopify storefront builder, Sandbox storage, and a skills API; chip stocks slide as NVIDIA teases its first consumer CPU in a decade
summary: A loaded few days for the web-platform stack: three Vercel drops, Deno 2.8.2 with post-quantum crypto, a semiconductor sell-off into Computex, a $750M Ramp round, and a 2.6M-record dental breach.

title: A hot jobs report triggers the year's worst market day; Stanley Cup heads to Vegas
summary: May payrolls came in at 172,000 — roughly double expectations — pushing bond yields up and sending the S&P 500 to its worst day since October and the Nasdaq down 4%; the FOMC meets June 16–17 under new Chair Kevin Warsh, and the Stanley Cup Final shifts to Las Vegas for Game 3.

title: Fallback models keep your sessions alive when Opus is overloaded
summary: 2.1.166 lets you chain up to three fallback models on overload and auto-retries once on the fallback; 2.1.167 is bug fixes only.

title: Anthropic floats a coordinated slowdown as Claude writes most of its own code; Alibaba undercuts on a 1M-token model
summary: Anthropic says more than 80% of code merged in May was written by Claude and called for coordinated restraint if self-improvement accelerates; Alibaba shipped a 1M-token multimodal model at $2 per million tokens; and the AI rally cracked, with the Nasdaq down 4% on Friday.

title: Cheap AI, a Next.js scramble, and a vibe-coding flood — six ways to get paid
summary: Launch edition — this week AI got cheaper and writes most of its own code, a Next.js security release put every SMB site on notice, and non-engineers are shipping half-built apps faster than they can fix them. Six moves, ranked by how fast and cheaply you can act.

title: Supabase hits $10.5B on vibe-coding; Quantinuum's $1.68B IPO and a supply-chain security scare
summary: Supabase reached a $10.5B valuation on the back of natural-language app building, Honeywell's Quantinuum raised $1.68B in a Nasdaq debut, Chinese-made PCBs under Nvidia AI chips drew national-security scrutiny, GitLab cut 14% of staff, and US data-center buildouts are slipping on grid constraints.

title: Jobs hold up, gas hits a four-year high, and the largest Starship flies: 60 days in the US
summary: Summer kickoff edition — a neutral, a-political roundup of the biggest US happenings of the past two months: a steady labor market against four-year-high gas prices, a new Fed chair, the largest Starship's debut, a measles surge, and record days in sports and at the box office.

title: A quiet stabilization window: hook feedback loops and headless reliability are the only things worth touching
summary: After last week's big feature run, the only new releases are a fix-heavy 2.1.163 and a pure-stabilization 2.1.165 — the items that matter are hooks that feed context back and a headless claude -p hang fix.

title: Sixty days that reset the AI stack: a near-$1T Anthropic, Opus 4.8, and coding agents writing their own code
summary: Summer kickoff edition — the past two months brought a nearly $1T Anthropic raise, a wave of cheaper and more agentic frontier models, reasoning-grade voice APIs, and the clearest signal yet that coding agents now write most of their own code.

title: The IPO window reopened and the PC got a new challenger: 60 days in tech
summary: Summer kickoff edition — SpaceX filed the largest IPO ever, Nvidia attacked the laptop CPU market, the tech IPO window blew open, your Next.js stack got a 400%-faster dev loop and a patch-now security bundle, and the year's biggest breach hit the SaaS identity layer.

title: Claude Code ships agent swarms, Opus 4.8, and one-command code cleanup
summary: Eleven releases since May 27 land dynamic multi-agent workflows, the Opus 4.8 default, and review tooling that now writes its own fixes — the most build-leverage update run in months.
